wezterm.on("gui-startup", function()

end)

-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================

config.tab_max_width = 40

local function get_path_from_uri(uri)
	if not uri then
		return nil
	end

	if type(uri) == "userdata" and uri.file_path then
		return uri.file_path
	end

	local s = tostring(uri)
	local path = s:gsub("^file://[^/]*", "")
	path = path:gsub("%%(%x%x)", function(hex)
		return string.char(tonumber(hex, 16))
	end)

	if path == "" then
		return nil
	end

	return path
end

local function shorten_path(path)
	if not path or path == "" then
		return nil
	end

	local home = os.getenv("HOME")

	if home and path == home then
		return "~"
	end

	if home and path:sub(1, #home + 1) == home .. "/" then
		path = "~" .. path:sub(#home + 1)
	end

	local parts = {}
	for p in path:gmatch("[^/]+") do
		table.insert(parts, p)
	end

	if path:sub(1, 1) == "~" then
		if #parts == 1 then
			return "~"
		elseif #parts >= 2 then
			return "~/" .. parts[#parts - 1] .. "/" .. parts[#parts]
		end
	end

	if #parts >= 2 then
		return parts[#parts - 1] .. "/" .. parts[#parts]
	end

	return parts[#parts] or path
end

local function get_cwd(tab)
	local pane = tab.active_pane
	if not pane then
		return "?"
	end

	local path = get_path_from_uri(pane.current_working_dir)
	local short = shorten_path(path)
	if short and short ~= "" then
		return short
	end

	local proc = pane.foreground_process_name
	if proc and proc ~= "" then
		local name = proc:gsub(".*[/\\]", "")
		if name ~= "" then
			return name
		end
	end

	return "?"
end

wezterm.on("format-tab-title", function(tab, _, _, _, _, max_width)
	local cwd = get_cwd(tab)
	local title = (tab.tab_title and #tab.tab_title > 0) and tab.tab_title or cwd

	local budget = math.max(8, math.min(max_width - 6, 40))
	if wezterm.column_width(title) > budget then
		title = wezterm.truncate_left(title, budget)
	end

	local tab_bg = tab.is_active and c.bg or c.bg_dark
	local tab_fg = tab.is_active and c.fg or c.comment
	local idx_fg = tab.is_active and c.blue or c.dark3

	return {
		{ Background = { Color = tab_bg } },
		{ Foreground = { Color = idx_fg } },
		{ Attribute = { Intensity = tab.is_active and "Bold" or "Normal" } },
		{ Text = " " .. tostring(tab.tab_index + 1) .. " " },

		{ Background = { Color = tab_bg } },
		{ Foreground = { Color = tab_fg } },
		{ Attribute = { Intensity = "Normal" } },
		{ Text = title .. " " },
	}
end)

-- ============================================================
-- Status bar
-- ============================================================

local function git_branch(pane)
	local cwd_uri = pane:get_current_working_dir()
	if not cwd_uri then
		return nil
	end

	local cwd = get_path_from_uri(cwd_uri)
	if not cwd then
		return nil
	end

	local path = cwd
	for _ = 1, 8 do
		local f = io.open(path .. "/.git/HEAD", "r")
		if f then
			local head = f:read("*l") or ""
			f:close()
			return head:match("ref: refs/heads/(.+)") or head:sub(1, 7)
		end

		local parent = path:match("^(.*)/[^/]+$")
		if not parent or parent == path then
			break
		end
		path = parent
	end

	return nil
end

wezterm.on("update-status", function(window, pane)
	local left = {}

	local ws = window:active_workspace()
	local ws_label = (ws == "default") and "default" or ws

	table.insert(left, { Background = { Color = c.blue } })
	table.insert(left, { Foreground = { Color = c.bg_dark } })
	table.insert(left, { Attribute = { Intensity = "Bold" } })
	table.insert(left, { Text = "  " .. ws_label .. "  " })

	if window:leader_is_active() then
		table.insert(left, { Background = { Color = c.bg_dark } })
		table.insert(left, { Foreground = { Color = c.orange } })
		table.insert(left, { Text = " WAIT " })
	end

	window:set_left_status(wezterm.format(left))

	local right = {}

	-- git branch first
	local branch = git_branch(pane)
	if branch then
		table.insert(right, { Background = { Color = c.bg_hl } })
		table.insert(right, { Foreground = { Color = c.orange } })
		table.insert(right, { Text = " 󰊢 " })
		table.insert(right, { Foreground = { Color = c.fg_dark } })
		table.insert(right, { Text = branch .. " " })
	end

	-- battery second
	local bat_info = wezterm.battery_info()
	if bat_info and #bat_info > 0 then
		local bat = bat_info[1]
		local pct = math.floor(bat.state_of_charge * 100 + 0.5)

		local bat_icon, bat_color
		local charging = bat.state == "Charging" or bat.state == "Full"

		if charging then
			bat_icon = "󰂄"
			bat_color = c.green
		elseif pct >= 80 then
			bat_icon = "󰁹"
			bat_color = c.green
		elseif pct >= 60 then
			bat_icon = "󰂁"
			bat_color = c.green
		elseif pct >= 40 then
			bat_icon = "󰁾"
			bat_color = c.yellow
		elseif pct >= 20 then
			bat_icon = "󰁼"
			bat_color = c.orange
		else
			bat_icon = "󰁺"
			bat_color = c.red
		end

		table.insert(right, { Background = { Color = c.bg_hl } })
		table.insert(right, { Foreground = { Color = bat_color } })
		table.insert(right, { Text = " " .. bat_icon .. " " })
		table.insert(right, { Foreground = { Color = c.fg_dark } })
		table.insert(right, { Text = pct .. "% " })
	end

	-- time last
	table.insert(right, { Background = { Color = c.blue } })
	table.insert(right, { Foreground = { Color = c.bg_dark } })
	table.insert(right, { Attribute = { Intensity = "Bold" } })
	table.insert(right, { Text = " 󰥔 " .. wezterm.strftime("%H:%M") .. " " })

	window:set_right_status(wezterm.format(right))
end)

	},
	{
		event = { Down = { streak = 1, button = { WheelUp = 1 } } },
		mods = "CTRL",
		action = act.IncreaseFontSize,
	},
	{
		event = { Down = { streak = 1, button = { WheelDown = 1 } } },
		mods = "CTRL",
		action = act.DecreaseFontSize,
	},
}

-- ============================================================
-- Keybindings
-- tmux-like leader workflow
-- ============================================================

-- tmux prefix equivalent: `
-- Press `, then another key from the bindings below.
config.leader = { key = "`", mods = "NONE", timeout_milliseconds = 1000 }
config.disable_default_key_bindings = true

local function is_vim(pane)
	return (pane:get_foreground_process_name() or ""):find("n?vim") ~= nil
end

local pane_direction = {
	h = "Left",
	j = "Down",
	k = "Up",
	l = "Right",
}

local function leader_nav(key)
	return {
		key = key,
		mods = "LEADER",
		action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, pane)
			-- Keep tmux-like pane movement, but do not steal the key from vim/nvim.
			if is_vim(pane) then
				win:perform_action(act.SendKey({ key = key, mods = "LEADER" }), pane)
			else
				win:perform_action(act.ActivatePaneDirection(pane_direction[key]), pane)
			end
		end),
	}
end

local function resize_pane(key, direction)
	return {
		key = key,
		mods = "LEADER",
		action = act.AdjustPaneSize({ direction, 5 }),
	}
end

local function activate_tab(index)
	return { key = tostring(index), mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivateTab(index - 1) }
end

config.keys = {
	-- Send literal backtick, equivalent to tmux: bind ` send-prefix
	{ key = "`", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SendKey({ key = "`" }) },

	-- Reload WezTerm config, equivalent to tmux: prefix + r
	{
		key = "r",
		mods = "LEADER",
		action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, pane)
			win:perform_action(act.ReloadConfiguration, pane)
			win:toast_notification("WezTerm", "Config reloaded", nil, 2000)
		end),
	},

	-- New tab/window, equivalent to tmux windows
	{ key = "c", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SpawnTab("CurrentPaneDomain") },
	{ key = "n", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SpawnTab("CurrentPaneDomain") },
	{ key = "q", mods = "LEADER", action = act.CloseCurrentPane({ confirm = false }) },
	{ key = "Q", mods = "LEADER", action = act.CloseCurrentTab({ confirm = false }) },

	-- Splits: tmux-compatible keys
	{ key = '"', mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.SplitVertical({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
	{ key = "%", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.SplitHorizontal({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
	{ key = "H", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.SplitHorizontal({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
	{ key = "|", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.SplitHorizontal({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
	{ key = "V", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.SplitVertical({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
	{ key = "-", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SplitVertical({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },

	-- Vim-like pane movement
	leader_nav("h"),
	leader_nav("j"),
	leader_nav("k"),
	leader_nav("l"),

	-- Resize panes, equivalent to tmux prefix + arrows
	resize_pane("LeftArrow", "Left"),
	resize_pane("RightArrow", "Right"),
	resize_pane("UpArrow", "Up"),
	resize_pane("DownArrow", "Down"),

	-- Pane utilities
	{ key = "z", mods = "LEADER", action = act.TogglePaneZoomState },
	{ key = "s", mods = "LEADER", action = act.PaneSelect({ mode = "SwapWithActive" }) },
	{ key = "p", mods = "LEADER", action = act.PaneSelect({}) },
	{ key = "o", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivatePaneDirection("Next") },

	-- Toggle tab/status bar, equivalent to tmux prefix + S
	{
		key = "S",
		mods = "LEADER|SHIFT",
		action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win)
			local overrides = win:get_config_overrides() or {}
			overrides.enable_tab_bar = not (overrides.enable_tab_bar == false)
			win:set_config_overrides(overrides)
		end),
	},

	-- Tab navigation, equivalent to tmux windows
	{ key = "Tab", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivateLastTab },
	{ key = "h", mods = "LEADER|CTRL", action = act.ActivateTabRelative(-1) },
	{ key = "l", mods = "LEADER|CTRL", action = act.ActivateTabRelative(1) },
	{ key = "{", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.MoveTabRelative(-1) },
	{ key = "}", mods = "LEADER|SHIFT", action = act.MoveTabRelative(1) },

	activate_tab(1),
	activate_tab(2),
	activate_tab(3),
	activate_tab(4),
	activate_tab(5),
	activate_tab(6),
	activate_tab(7),
	activate_tab(8),
	activate_tab(9),

	-- Rename tab
	{
		key = ",",
		mods = "LEADER",
		action = act.PromptInputLine({
			description = "Rename tab:",
			action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, _, line)
				if line then
					win:active_tab():set_title(line)
				end
			end),
		}),
	},
	{
		key = "e",
		mods = "LEADER",
		action = act.PromptInputLine({
			description = "Rename tab:",
			action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, _, line)
				if line then
					win:active_tab():set_title(line)
				end
			end),
		}),
	},

	-- Workspaces: WezTerm-native replacement for tmux sessions
	{ key = "w", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ShowLauncherArgs({ flags = "WORKSPACES" }) },
	{
		key = "W",
		mods = "LEADER|SHIFT",
		action = act.PromptInputLine({
			description = "New workspace name:",
			action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, _, line)
				if line and #line > 0 then
					win:perform_action(act.SwitchToWorkspace({ name = line }), win:active_pane())
				end
			end),
		}),
	},

	-- Copy/search essentials, kept because default keybindings are disabled
	{ key = "c", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.CopyTo("Clipboard") },
	{ key = "v", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.PasteFrom("Clipboard") },
	{ key = "v", mods = "LEADER", action = act.PasteFrom("PrimarySelection") },
	{ key = "[", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivateCopyMode },
	{ key = "y", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivateCopyMode },
	{ key = "f", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.Search({ CaseSensitiveString = "" }) },
	{ key = "P", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.ActivateCommandPalette },

	-- Scrollback
	{ key = "u", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.ScrollByPage(-0.5) },
	{ key = "d", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.ScrollByPage(0.5) },
	{ key = "PageUp", mods = "SHIFT", action = act.ScrollByPage(-1) },
	{ key = "PageDown", mods = "SHIFT", action = act.ScrollByPage(1) },
	{ key = "Home", mods = "SHIFT", action = act.ScrollToTop },
	{ key = "End", mods = "SHIFT", action = act.ScrollToBottom },

	-- Font/window utilities
	{ key = "=", mods = "CTRL", action = act.IncreaseFontSize },
	{ key = "-", mods = "CTRL", action = act.DecreaseFontSize },
	{ key = "0", mods = "CTRL", action = act.ResetFontSize },
	{ key = "F11", mods = "NONE", action = act.ToggleFullScreen },
	{ key = "l", mods = "CTRL|SHIFT", action = act.ClearScrollback("ScrollbackAndViewport") },
	{ key = ".", mods = "LEADER", action = act.ShowDebugOverlay },
}

-- ============================================================
-- Copy mode
-- ============================================================

config.key_tables = {
	copy_mode = {
		{ key = "h", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveLeft") },
		{ key = "j", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveDown") },
		{ key = "k", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveUp") },
		{ key = "l", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveRight") },
		{ key = "w", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveForwardWord") },
		{ key = "b", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveBackwardWord") },
		{ key = "e", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveForwardWordEnd") },
		{ key = "0", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveToStartOfLine") },
		{ key = "$", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveToEndOfLineContent") },
		{ key = "g", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveToScrollbackTop") },
		{ key = "G", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("MoveToScrollbackBottom") },
		{ key = "u", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode({ MoveByPage = -0.5 }) },
		{ key = "d", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode({ MoveByPage = 0.5 }) },

		{ key = "v", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode({ SetSelectionMode = "Cell" }) },
		{ key = "V", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode({ SetSelectionMode = "Line" }) },
		{ key = "v", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode({ SetSelectionMode = "Block" }) },

		{
			key = "y",
			mods = "NONE",
			action = act.Multiple({ { CopyTo = "ClipboardAndPrimarySelection" }, { CopyMode = "Close" } }),
		},
		{
			key = "Enter",
			mods = "NONE",
			action = act.Multiple({ { CopyTo = "ClipboardAndPrimarySelection" }, { CopyMode = "Close" } }),
		},
		{ key = "Escape", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("Close") },
		{ key = "q", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("Close") },

		{ key = "/", mods = "NONE", action = act.Search({ CaseSensitiveString = "" }) },
		{ key = "n", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("NextMatch") },
		{ key = "N", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("PriorMatch") },
	},

	search_mode = {
		{ key = "Escape", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("Close") },
		{ key = "Enter", mods = "NONE", action = act.CopyMode("PriorMatch") },
		{ key = "n", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode("NextMatch") },
		{ key = "p", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode("PriorMatch") },
		{ key = "r", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode("CycleMatchType") },
		{ key = "u", mods = "CTRL", action = act.CopyMode("ClearPattern") },
	},
}

return config

diff --git a/wezterm/.wezterm.lua b/wezterm/.wezterm.lua
index 46a0977..073d894 100644
--- a/wezterm/.wezterm.lua
+++ b/wezterm/.wezterm.lua
@@ -70,9 +70,9 @@ config.enable_scroll_bar = false
 config.scrollback_lines = 10000
 
 config.window_padding = {
-	left = 8,
-	right = 8,
-	top = 6,
+	left = 0,
+	right = 0,
+	top = 0,
 	bottom = 0,
 }
 
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@ config.font = wezterm.font_with_fallback({
 	{ family = "Noto Color Emoji" },
 })
 
-config.font_size = 11
+config.font_size = 10.75
 config.line_height = 1.1
 config.cell_width = 1.0
 
@@ -465,85 +465,130 @@ config.mouse_bindings = {
 
 -- ============================================================
 -- Keybindings
+-- tmux-like leader workflow
 -- ============================================================
 
-config.leader = { key = "a", mods = "CTRL", timeout_milliseconds = 1000 }
+-- tmux prefix equivalent: `
+-- Press `, then another key from the bindings below.
+config.leader = { key = "`", mods = "NONE", timeout_milliseconds = 1000 }
 config.disable_default_key_bindings = true
 
 local function is_vim(pane)
 	return (pane:get_foreground_process_name() or ""):find("n?vim") ~= nil
 end
 
-local dir = { h = "Left", j = "Down", k = "Up", l = "Right" }
+local pane_direction = {
+	h = "Left",
+	j = "Down",
+	k = "Up",
+	l = "Right",
+}
 
-local function nav(resize, key)
-	local mods = resize and "ALT|SHIFT" or "ALT"
+local function leader_nav(key)
 	return {
 		key = key,
-		mods = mods,
+		mods = "LEADER",
 		action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, pane)
+			-- Keep tmux-like pane movement, but do not steal the key from vim/nvim.
 			if is_vim(pane) then
-				win:perform_action(act.SendKey({ key = key, mods = mods }), pane)
-			elseif resize then
-				win:perform_action(act.AdjustPaneSize({ dir[key], 3 }), pane)
+				win:perform_action(act.SendKey({ key = key, mods = "LEADER" }), pane)
 			else
-				win:perform_action(act.ActivatePaneDirection(dir[key]), pane)
+				win:perform_action(act.ActivatePaneDirection(pane_direction[key]), pane)
 			end
 		end),
 	}
 end
 
+local function resize_pane(key, direction)
+	return {
+		key = key,
+		mods = "LEADER",
+		action = act.AdjustPaneSize({ direction, 5 }),
+	}
+end
+
+local function activate_tab(index)
+	return { key = tostring(index), mods = "LEADER", action = act.ActivateTab(index - 1) }
+end
+
 config.keys = {
-	nav(false, "h"),
-	nav(false, "j"),
-	nav(false, "k"),
-	nav(false, "l"),
-
-	nav(true, "h"),
-	nav(true, "j"),
-	nav(true, "k"),
-	nav(true, "l"),
-
-	{ key = "\\", mods = "ALT", action = act.SplitHorizontal({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
-	{ key = "-", mods = "ALT", action = act.SplitVertical({ domain = "CurrentPaneDomain" }) },
+	-- Send literal backtick, equivalent to tmux: bind ` send-prefix
+	{ key = "`", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SendKey({ key = "`" }) },
+
+	-- Reload WezTerm config, equivalent to tmux: prefix + r
 	{
-		key = "|",
-		mods = "ALT|SHIFT",
-		action = act.SplitPane({ top_level = true, direction = "Right", size = { Percent = 50 } }),
+		key = "r",
+		mods = "LEADER",
+		action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, pane)
+			win:perform_action(act.ReloadConfiguration, pane)
+			win:toast_notification("WezTerm", "Config reloaded", nil, 2000)
+		end),
 	},
+
+	-- New tab/window, equivalent to tmux windows
+	{ key = "c", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SpawnTab("CurrentPaneDomain") },
+	{ key = "n", mods = "LEADER", action = act.SpawnTab("CurrentPaneDomain") },
+	{ key = "q", mods = "LEADER", action = act.CloseCurrentPane({ confirm = false }) },
+	{ key = "Q", mods = "LEADER", action = act.CloseCurrentTab({ confirm = false }) },
+

content.thinking:
**Deciding on edits**

I need to make some decisions about edits. I think I could add a Performance section after the general one, and also consider removing a duplicate Wayland block. There's that repeated "false" setting, and I’m wondering if we should keep it because if the user is using Hyprland, it might need to have `enable_wayland=false` for specific fixes. But pushing for X11 could degrade performance, while switching to Wayland might introduce issues like clipboard lag. The user mentioned lags, so maybe I should change that to "true" but add a note to revert if it’s unstable. Also, I see some repeated lines that should be cleaned up!

**Clarifying configuration settings**

I think I'm confirming some configurations. For `front_end`, it accepts "OpenGL," "WebGpu," or "Software." The `max_fps` defaults to 60. The `animation_fps` might control the blinking cursor, so I'd probably set that closer to 10 instead of 1, especially if `cursor_blink_rate` is set to 0.

The `status_update_interval` likely operates in milliseconds, maybe starting at 1000, but I want to set it to 5000 for updates. I believe `enable_wayland` should default to true, but I noticed a duplicate that I'll change to true. Lastly, for `scrollback_lines`, I could lower it to 5000 since it only affects non-alternate screens.

**Evaluating status update options**

I'm considering if adding a status update could still cause a micro stutter. A 5-second interval may not be significant. I think about possibly adding a `status_update_interval`, but then I wonder about enabling the tab bar with `config.enable_tab_bar = true?`, but that doesn't seem necessary. I realize I need to validate the syntax without WezTerm. Should I use Lua? I wonder if it's installed—though WezTerm config uses `require("wezterm")`, which I can't access right now.

I need to make some decisions about edits. I think I could add a Performance section after the general one, and also consider removing a duplicate Wayland block. There's that repeated "false" setting, and I’m wondering if we should keep it because if the user is using Hyprland, it might need to have `enable_wayland=false` for specific fixes. But pushing for X11 could degrade performance, while switching to Wayland might introduce issues like clipboard lag. The user mentioned lags, so maybe I should change that to "true" but add a note to revert if it’s unstable. Also, I see some repeated lines that should be cleaned up!"},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Clarifying configuration settings**

I think I'm confirming some configurations. For `front_end`, it accepts "OpenGL," "WebGpu," or "Software." The `max_fps` defaults to 60. The `animation_fps` might control the blinking cursor, so I'd probably set that closer to 10 instead of 1, especially if `cursor_blink_rate` is set to 0.

The `status_update_interval` likely operates in milliseconds, maybe starting at 1000, but I want to set it to 5000 for updates. I believe `enable_wayland` should default to true, but I noticed a duplicate that I'll change to true. Lastly, for `scrollback_lines`, I could lower it to 5000 since it only affects non-alternate screens."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Evaluating status update options**

I'm considering if adding a status update could still cause a micro stutter. A 5-second interval may not be significant. I think about possibly adding a `status_update_interval`, but then I wonder about enabling the tab bar with `config.enable_tab_bar = true?`, but that doesn't seem necessary. I realize I need to validate the syntax without WezTerm. Should I use Lua? I wonder if it's installed—though WezTerm config uses `require("wezterm")`, which I can't access right now."}]}

edits.newText:
-- Prefer the native Wayland path on Hyprland/Wayland; forcing XWayland can
-- make rapid Neovim redraws feel noticeably less responsive.
config.enable_wayland = true
config.check_for_updates = false
config.show_update_window = false
config.warn_about_missing_glyphs = false
config.audible_bell = "Disabled"

-- ============================================================
-- Performance
-- ============================================================

-- WebGPU is usually smoother for full-screen terminal redraws such as
-- scrolling huge Neovim buffers. HighPerformance avoids integrated-GPU
-- downclocking when available.
config.front_end = "WebGpu"
config.webgpu_power_preference = "HighPerformance"

-- Keep redraws responsive on high-refresh monitors while avoiding needless
-- animation work. Cursor blinking is disabled to reduce periodic repaints.
config.max_fps = 120
config.animation_fps = 1
config.cursor_blink_rate = 0
config.default_cursor_style = "SteadyBlock"

-- The status callback checks git/battery state; update it less often so it
-- does not compete with Neovim during heavy redraws.
config.status_update_interval = 5000

-- Disable ligature shaping for lower text-layout overhead in dense code views.
config.harfbuzz_features = { "calt=0", "clig=0", "liga=0" }

cyan_b = "#a4daff",
	green_b = "#9fe044",
	yellow_b = "#faba4a",
	magenta_b = "#c7a9ff",
	red_b = "#ff899d",
}

-- ============================================================
-- General
-- ============================================================

-- Prefer the native Wayland path on Hyprland/Wayland; forcing XWayland can
-- make rapid Neovim redraws feel noticeably less responsive.
config.enable_wayland = true
config.check_for_updates = false
config.show_update_window = false
config.warn_about_missing_glyphs = false
config.audible_bell = "Disabled"

-- ============================================================
-- Performance
-- ============================================================

-- WebGPU is usually smoother for full-screen terminal redraws such as
-- scrolling huge Neovim buffers. HighPerformance avoids integrated-GPU
-- downclocking when available.
config.front_end = "WebGpu"
config.webgpu_power_preference = "HighPerformance"

-- Keep redraws responsive on high-refresh monitors while avoiding needless
-- animation work. Cursor blinking is disabled to reduce periodic repaints.
config.max_fps = 120
config.animation_fps = 1
config.cursor_blink_rate = 0
config.default_cursor_style = "SteadyBlock"

-- The status callback checks git/battery state; update it less often so it
-- does not compete with Neovim during heavy redraws.
config.status_update_interval = 5000

-- Disable ligature shaping for lower text-layout overhead in dense code views.
config.harfbuzz_features = { "calt=0", "clig=0", "liga=0" }

config.window_close_confirmation = "NeverPrompt"
config.skip_close_confirmation_for_processes_named = {
	"bash",
	"zsh",
	"fish",
	"sh",
	"nu",
}

-- ============================================================
-- Window
-- ============================================================

config.window_decorations = "NONE"
config.window_background_opacity = 1.0
config.initial_cols = 120
config.initial_rows = 30
config.enable_scroll_bar = false
config.scrollback_lines = 10000

config.window_padding = {
	left = 0,
	right = 0,
	top = 0,
	bottom = 0,
}

config.inactive_pane_hsb = {
	saturation = 0.95,
	brightness = 0.8,
}

wezterm.on("gui-startup", function()
	local _, _, window = mux.spawn_window({})
	window:gui_window():maximize()
end)

Wez's Terminal Emulator
http://github.com/wez/wezterm

Usage: wezterm [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Commands:
  start                  Start the GUI, optionally running an alternative program [aliases: -e]
  ssh                    Establish an ssh session
  serial                 Open a serial port
  connect                Connect to wezterm multiplexer
  ls-fonts               Display information about fonts
  show-keys              Show key assignments
  cli                    Interact with experimental mux server
  imgcat                 Output an image to the terminal
  set-working-directory  Advise the terminal of the current working directory by emitting an OSC
                             7 escape sequence
  record                 Record a terminal session as an asciicast
  replay                 Replay an asciicast terminal session
  shell-completion       Generate shell completion information
  help                   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -n, --skip-config                Skip loading wezterm.lua
      --config-file <CONFIG_FILE>  Specify the configuration file to use, overrides the normal
                                   configuration file resolution
      --config <name=value>        Override specific configuration values

wezterm/.wezterm.lua | 241 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-------------------
 1 file changed, 152 insertions(+), 89 deletions(-)
3:--- a/wezterm/.wezterm.lua
9:-config.enable_wayland = false
10:+-- Prefer the native Wayland path on Hyprland/Wayland; forcing XWayland can
11:+-- make rapid Neovim redraws feel noticeably less responsive.
12:+config.enable_wayland = true
18:+-- ============================================================
19:+-- Performance
20:+-- ============================================================
22:+-- WebGPU is usually smoother for full-screen terminal redraws such as
23:+-- scrolling huge Neovim buffers. HighPerformance avoids integrated-GPU
24:+-- downclocking when available.
25:+config.front_end = "WebGpu"
26:+config.webgpu_power_preference = "HighPerformance"
28:+-- Keep redraws responsive on high-refresh monitors while avoiding needless
29:+-- animation work. Cursor blinking is disabled to reduce periodic repaints.
30:+config.max_fps = 120
31:+config.animation_fps = 1
32:+config.cursor_blink_rate = 0
33:+config.default_cursor_style = "SteadyBlock"
35:+-- The status callback checks git/battery state; update it less often so it
36:+-- does not compete with Neovim during heavy redraws.
37:+config.status_update_interval = 5000
39:+-- Disable ligature shaping for lower text-layout overhead in dense code views.
40:+config.harfbuzz_features = { "calt=0", "clig=0", "liga=0" }
62:-config.font_size = 11
63:+config.font_size = 10.75
71:--- Hyprland/Wayland specific fixes
72:-config.enable_wayland = false
73:-config.window_decorations = "NONE"
74:+-- Hyprland/Wayland decorations are configured above.
80:-config.hyperlink_rules = wezterm.default_hyperlink_rules()
81:+-- Avoid the default URL regex set scanning every rendered line. Keep only the
82:+-- explicit rules below; Neovim can still handle most links/files with gx/gF.
83:+config.hyperlink_rules = {}
91:+-- tmux-like leader workflow
94:-config.leader = { key = "a", mods = "CTRL", timeout_milliseconds = 1000 }
95:+-- tmux prefix equivalent: `
96:+-- Press `, then another key from the bindings below.
97:+config.leader = { key = "`", mods = "NONE", timeout_milliseconds = 1000 }

vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("TermOpen", {
  pattern = "term://*",
  callback = function(args)
    local bufname = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(args.buf)

  end,
})

**Considering UI optimization**

I think the WezTerm status is part of the tab bar. If the tab bar is hidden, then the `set_left_status` wouldn’t show up, which could streamline the UI a bit. Disabling the tab bar completely might not be ideal, as users may want tabs. There’s also a concern about the frequent calls to `format-tab-title`, which could impact performance. Testing with a static tab bar or toggling a performance mode may help. Plus, I wonder about removing hyperlink rules entirely for better efficiency.

**Analyzing terminal performance**

When dealing with large files in Neovim, I've noticed that terminal scrolling in alt-screen mode can cause redraw issues. Key factors contributing to this include Treesitter, LSP, diagnostics, relative numbers, and cursor line highlights. These elements seem to impact performance significantly during scrolling. I wonder if addressing these factors could improve the user experience when working with large files. It might be worth exploring ways to optimize the setup for smoother performance overall.

-- scrolling huge Neovim buffers. HighPerformance avoids integrated-GPU
-- downclocking when available.

config.webgpu_power_preference = "HighPerformance"

-- Keep redraws responsive on high-refresh monitors while avoiding needless
-- animation work. Cursor blinking is disabled to reduce periodic repaints.
config.max_fps = 120

config.front_end = "OpenGL"
config.webgpu_power_preference = "HighPerformance"

I changed `wezterm/.wezterm.lua` more aggressively:

- Switched renderer from `WebGpu` → `OpenGL`

- Disabled WezTerm tab/status bar by default to reduce redraw overhead
- Changed font render target from `HorizontalLcd` → `Normal`

Important: fully quit and restart WezTerm. A reload may not apply renderer/Wayland changes.

If it’s still laggy after restart, try this one-line fallback next:

```lua
config.enable_wayland = false
```

content.text:
Brought the tab/status bar back in `wezterm/.wezterm.lua`:

```lua
config.enable_tab_bar = true
config.hide_tab_bar_if_only_one_tab = false
```

Difference between `HorizontalLcd` and `Normal`:

- `HorizontalLcd`: subpixel LCD antialiasing. Text can look sharper on some LCD screens, but it’s more expensive and can look weird on rotated/scaled/HiDPI displays.
- `Normal`: grayscale antialiasing. Usually slightly softer, but simpler and often smoother/faster for terminal redraws.

For Neovim scrolling performance, `Normal` is the safer choice. If you strongly prefer sharper font rendering, you can switch it back:

```lua
config.freetype_render_target = "HorizontalLcd"
```
